authors: Kayo Mimizuka Katlyn Glover Inga K. Trauthig Samuel C. Woolley
raw_title: “I Don’t Even Watch News (Voluntarily) on WeChat” – Socially-Compelled Exposure to Misinformation Among U.S. Diaspora Communities
title_en: “I Don’t Even Watch News (Voluntarily) on WeChat” – Socially-Compelled Exposure to Misinformation Among U.S. Diaspora Communities
title_zh: “我甚至（自愿地）不在微信上看新闻”——美国侨民社区中社会性被迫接触错误信息
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了美国侨民社区成员如何通过微信等社交媒体平台，非自愿地接触到错误信息。研究发现，即使人们不想看新闻，也可能因为社交压力或平台算法而被迫看到虚假内容。
plain_summary_en: This study examines how members of U.S. diaspora communities are involuntarily exposed to misinformation through social media platforms like WeChat. The research finds that even when people don't want to see news, they may be forced to encounter false information due to social pressure or platform algorithms.
research_direction_zh: 研究关注数字时代中，社交媒体如何影响侨民社区的信息接触模式，特别是非自愿接触错误信息的现象及其社会影响。
research_direction_en: The research focuses on how social media influences information exposure patterns in diaspora communities in the digital age, particularly the phenomenon of involuntary exposure to misinformation and its social implications.
